---
title: "Core_drought_metabolome"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
## setup 

library(tidyverse)
library(openxlsx)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

dir.create("~/WUR/core_drought_metabolome", recursive = TRUE)

PADJ_THRESHOLD <- 0.05
CORE_THRESHOLD <- 4   


## ---- 1 - load and combine pos and neg mode data ----

pos <- read.xlsx(
  "~/Downloads/OneDrive_1_06-05-2026/Analysis_chrats_2026/metabolomics_limma_perspp_all_spp.xlsx",
  sheet = "positive_per_species"
)
neg <- read.xlsx(
  "~/Downloads/OneDrive_1_06-05-2026/Analysis_chrats_2026/metabolomics_limma_perspp_all_spp.xlsx",
  sheet = "negative_per_species"
)

# track which mode each feature came from
pos$ionization_mode <- "positive"
neg$ionization_mode <- "negative"

# combine into one table of ~7275 features
metabolites <- rbind(pos, neg)

cat("Positive mode features:", nrow(pos), "\n")
cat("Negative mode features:", nrow(neg), "\n")
cat("Combined features:     ", nrow(metabolites), "\n")


## 2. extract species list 

species_list       <- gsub("\\.\\-\\.logFC", "",
                           colnames(metabolites)[grep("\\.\\-\\.logFC$", colnames(metabolites))])
species_list_clean <- gsub("\\.", " ", species_list)

cat("\nSpecies found:", length(species_list), "\n")
cat("First:", species_list_clean[1], "\n")
cat("Last: ", species_list_clean[length(species_list_clean)], "\n")


## 3. count enriched/depleted species per metabolite

count_drought_species <- function(df, species, padj_thr) {

  n_enriched <- integer(nrow(df))
  n_depleted <- integer(nrow(df))

  for (sp in species) {
    logfc_col <- paste0(sp, ".-.logFC")
    padj_col  <- paste0(sp, ".-.adj.P.Val")

    if (!(logfc_col %in% colnames(df)) | !(padj_col %in% colnames(df))) next

    logfc <- as.numeric(df[[logfc_col]])
    padj  <- as.numeric(df[[padj_col]])

    n_enriched <- n_enriched + as.integer(!is.na(padj) & padj < padj_thr & logfc < 0)
    n_depleted <- n_depleted + as.integer(!is.na(padj) & padj < padj_thr & logfc > 0)
  }

  df$n_enriched <- n_enriched
  df$n_depleted <- n_depleted
  return(df)
}

metabolites <- count_drought_species(metabolites, species_list, PADJ_THRESHOLD)

cat("\nMax enriched in:", max(metabolites$n_enriched), "species\n")
cat("Max depleted in:", max(metabolites$n_depleted), "species\n")
cat("\nMetabolites enriched in >=5 species:", sum(metabolites$n_enriched >= CORE_THRESHOLD), "\n")
cat("Metabolites depleted in >=5 species:", sum(metabolites$n_depleted >= CORE_THRESHOLD), "\n")
```

```{r}
## 4. distribution of how many species each metabolite is responsive in

counts_combined <- bind_rows(
  data.frame(n = metabolites$n_enriched, direction = "drought-enriched"),
  data.frame(n = metabolites$n_depleted, direction = "drought-depleted")
) %>% 
  filter(n > 0) %>%
  mutate(direction = factor(direction, 
                            levels = c("drought-enriched", "drought-depleted")))

p_dist <- ggplot(counts_combined, aes(x = n, fill = direction)) + 
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, position = "identity", alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:max(counts_combined$n)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("drought-enriched" = "#D73027", 
                               "drought-depleted" = "#4575B4")) +
  labs(
    title = "How many species is each metabolite drought-responsive in?",
    x     = "Number of species with significant DR vs. WW response",
    y     = "Number of metabolites",
    fill  = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(text = element_text(size = 13))

#Histogram where each bar showas how many metabolites are significant in exactly how many species

print(p_dist)

# exact counts per n
cat("Drought-ENRICHED metabolites per number of species:\n")
print(table(metabolites$n_enriched[metabolites$n_enriched > 0]))

cat("\nDrought-DEPLETED metabolites per number of species:\n")
print(table(metabolites$n_depleted[metabolites$n_depleted > 0]))

#By setting the core threshold = 4, we have 15 drought-enriched and 2 drought-depleted core metabolites across the 7275 features.(less than 0.3%)
```

```{r}
## 5. Identify core drought metabolites
 
core_enriched <- metabolites[metabolites$n_enriched >= CORE_THRESHOLD,]
core_depleted <- metabolites[metabolites$n_depleted >= CORE_THRESHOLD,]

#sort by decreasing n_species
core_enriched <- core_enriched[order(core_enriched$n_enriched),]
core_depleted <- core_depleted[order(-core_depleted$n_depleted),]
# show who they are
cat("=== CORE DROUGHT-ENRICHED (n >=", CORE_THRESHOLD, "species) ===\n")
print(core_enriched[, c("feature.ID", "ionization_mode", "n_enriched",
                         "ms2query_first_annotation",
                         "ms2query_first_annotation_superclass",
                         "sirius_first_annotation",
                         "gnps_first_annotation")])

cat("\n=== CORE DROUGHT-DEPLETED (n >=", CORE_THRESHOLD, "species) ===\n")
print(core_depleted[, c("feature.ID", "ionization_mode", "n_depleted",
                         "ms2query_first_annotation",
                         "ms2query_first_annotation_superclass",
                         "sirius_first_annotation",
                         "gnps_first_annotation")])
core_met_table_dep <- core_depleted[, c("feature.ID", "ionization_mode", "n_depleted",
                         "ms2query_first_annotation",
                         "ms2query_first_annotation_superclass",
                         "sirius_first_annotation",
                         "gnps_first_annotation")]
core_met_table_enr <- core_enriched[, c("feature.ID", "ionization_mode", "n_enriched",
                         "ms2query_first_annotation",
                         "ms2query_first_annotation_superclass",
                         "sirius_first_annotation",
                         "gnps_first_annotation")]

```

```{r}
## 6. bubble plot of core drought metabolites
# combine enriched and depleted into one plotting dataframe
core_enriched$direction <- "drought-enriched"
core_enriched$n_species  <- core_enriched$n_enriched
core_depleted$direction  <- "drought-depleted"
core_depleted$n_species  <- core_depleted$n_depleted

bubble_df <- bind_rows(
  core_enriched[, c("feature.ID", "ionization_mode", "n_species",
                    "direction", "ms2query_first_annotation",
                    "ms2query_first_annotation_superclass")],
  core_depleted[, c("feature.ID", "ionization_mode", "n_species",
                    "direction", "ms2query_first_annotation",
                    "ms2query_first_annotation_superclass")])
  
# replace NA annotations with "unannotated"
bubble_df$ms2query_first_annotation_superclass[
  is.na(bubble_df$ms2query_first_annotation_superclass)] <- "unannotated"

# create a label: use ms2query annotation if available, otherwise feature ID
bubble_df$label <- ifelse(
  !is.na(bubble_df$ms2query_first_annotation),
  bubble_df$ms2query_first_annotation,
  as.character(bubble_df$feature.ID)
)

# sort by direction then n_species for clean y-axis ordering
bubble_df <- bubble_df %>%
  arrange(direction, n_species) %>%
  mutate(label = factor(label, levels = unique(label)))

# bubble plot

p_bubble <- ggplot(bubble_df, aes(
    x     = n_species,
    y     = label,
    color = ms2query_first_annotation_superclass
  )) +
  geom_point(size = 5, alpha = 0.85) + 
  facet_grid(direction ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:max(bubble_df$n_species)) +
  labs(
    title = "Core drought metabolome",
    x     = "Number of species with significant response",
    y     = NULL,
    color = "Chemical class\n(MS2Query)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    text       = element_text(size = 13),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

print(p_bubble)

```

```{r}
ggsave(
  plot     = p_bubble,
  filename = "~/WUR//bubble_plot_core_metabolites.png",
  width    = 3500, height = 2000, units = "px"
)
```
